meta.scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan (PM-KUSUM)
meta.ministry: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE), Government of India
notes: PM-KUSUM is structured in three components (A, B, C) launched in 2019, with PM-KUSUM 2.0 enhancements in 2024.
Subsidy structure varies by state. UP-specific data reflects UPNEDA implementation rules.
Benchmark costs are MNRE-fixed for subsidy calculation; actual market prices may vary ±15%.
Bank loan portion typically through NABARD-channelled banks (PNB, SBI, BoB) — subject to credit appraisal.

subsidy_structure_up.notes: UP follows the standard 30/30/40 split. Of the farmer's 40%, up to 30% can be bank loan and 10% must be own funds.

component_a.name: Component A — Decentralised Ground-Mounted Solar Power Plants
component_a.short_description: Install 500 kW to 2 MW solar plants on barren/non-cultivable land. Sell power to DISCOM. Earn monthly income.
component_a.subsidy_basis: Per-MW capex, with PPA-based monthly earnings rather than direct subsidy
process_steps: Apply on PM-KUSUM portal (pmkusum.mnre.gov.in) or UPNEDA office
UPNEDA verifies land ownership + substation proximity
DISCOM signs PPA at MNRE-set feed-in tariff
Vendor empanelled by MNRE installs plant (12-18 months)
Monthly DISCOM payment direct to farmer bank account
component_a.best_fit_for: Landowner-farmers with 4-16 acres of barren/idle land within 5 km of a 33/11 kV substation. Land otherwise generating zero income.
deal_breakers: Land is cultivable or in use (would require diversion permissions)
More than 5 km from substation (transmission costs kill economics)

component_b.name: Component B — Standalone Solar Pumps
component_b.short_description: Install standalone (off-grid) solar pump for irrigation. No electricity connection needed. 60% subsidy.
component_b.subsidy_basis: Per-pump benchmark cost (MNRE-fixed by HP), with 30% central + 30% state subsidy

pump_options.suitable_for: Vegetable plots, small orchards, drip irrigation (0.5-1 acre)
8+ acre commercial farms, deep aquifers (>150 ft), water-intensive crops

process_steps: Apply on PM-KUSUM portal or UPNEDA district office
Submit land records, Aadhaar, bank details, water source proof
Subsidy disbursed direct to vendor (farmer pays only their 40% share)
component_b.best_fit_for: Farmers without reliable grid electricity for irrigation, especially in Bundelkhand and Terai regions of UP with frequent power cuts during sowing season.
deal_breakers: Already received KUSUM-B subsidy in past 5 years

component_c.name: Component C — Solarisation of Grid-Connected Agricultural Pumps
component_c.short_description: Add solar to your existing electric pump. Use solar power for irrigation. Sell excess to DISCOM. 60% subsidy.

sub_components.name: Individual Pump Solarisation
sub_components.description: Solar panels installed for a single farmer's existing grid-connected pump. Excess generation sold to DISCOM.

sub_components.name: Feeder-Level Solarisation
sub_components.description: Solar plant solarises an entire agricultural feeder serving multiple farmers. Implemented by DISCOM with farmer consent.

component_c.subsidy_basis: Per-kW of solar capacity added (sized to match pump load)
component_c.solar_sizing_logic: Solar capacity in kWp ≈ pump HP × 1.2 (to allow for excess generation)

earnings_logic.farmer_continues_to_pay_existing_pump_ta: Yes, but solar offsets most consumption

process_steps: Apply via UPNEDA portal with existing pump connection number
DISCOM verifies pump existence + consumption history
Solar pump operational + DISCOM credits monthly excess
component_c.best_fit_for: Farmers with reliable electricity but high pump bills (₹500+/month). Solar offsets 60% of consumption + earns from excess. Net financial benefit ~₹25-50K/year on 5-7.5 HP pumps.

ineligibility_scenarios.explanation: KUSUM A/B/C require land ownership documents. Tenant farmers ineligible unless landowner applies.
ineligibility_scenarios.alternative: Some states have lease-based variants; not yet in UP. Encourage landowner partnership.

ineligibility_scenarios.explanation: Component B not viable below 0.5 acres. Component A requires 4+ acres minimum.
ineligibility_scenarios.alternative: Consider PM Surya Ghar for residential rooftop solar instead.

ineligibility_scenarios.explanation: KUSUM Component B requires identifiable water source (borewell, well, tank). Without water, a pump is pointless.
ineligibility_scenarios.alternative: Drill borewell first, then apply. UPNEDA does not subsidize borewell drilling.

ineligibility_scenarios.scenario: Already received KUSUM subsidy in past 5 years
ineligibility_scenarios.explanation: Anti-double-subsidy rule. Same farmer cannot stack multiple KUSUM-B installations.
ineligibility_scenarios.alternative: Consider PM Surya Ghar rooftop on farmhouse — separate scheme, separate budget.

ineligibility_scenarios.scenario: Land more than 5 km from substation (for Component A only)
ineligibility_scenarios.explanation: Transmission costs make Component A uneconomic at distance.
ineligibility_scenarios.alternative: Component B works regardless of substation distance — pursue that path.

standard_priority: Established borewells with consistent water table
documents_required: Water source proof (borewell certificate or photograph + GPS coordinates)
Caste certificate (if SC/ST applying for quota slot)

kusum_specialist_vendor_requirements.different_from_rooftop_installers: Most rooftop solar installers cannot do KUSUM. Borewell expertise, pump fitting, VFD integration require specialist crews. MNRE maintains a separate empanelled-vendor list for KUSUM pumps.
